purpose: Full per-step hidden-activation stack for the R6c frozen 2022-2023 rollout (transparency gap closure).

architecture.policy_net: Linear(1834->256) ReLU -> Linear(256->128) ReLU -> Linear(128->64) ReLU

self_verification.interpretation: 0 => hooks captured the canonical forward exactly (shape-matched, not skipped)

naming_correction: The shipped r6c_frozen_2022_2023_rollout_package.npz 'test_hidden' is (N,128) -- it is the PENULTIMATE 128-d activation (policy_net.3.ReLU), not the 64-d final latent. policy_layers() mislabels it 'policy_latent_64' because it assumes a 2-Linear MLP. The TRUE 64-d final latent that feeds action_net is exported here as 'policy_net.5.ReLU'. The Stage-1 KMeans codebook was fit on the 128-d penultimate (self-consistent, but it is the penultimate, not the final, layer).
alignment: row i of every array corresponds to dates[i]; one row per internal trading day

action_mode.role: deterministic action (Beta/Dirichlet mode) fed to env.step

alpha_params.role: Dirichlet/Beta concentration params of the action distribution

policy_latent_penultimate_128.role: LEGACY policy_layers field mislabeled 'policy_latent_64'; really the 128-d penultimate (==policy_net.3.ReLU)

policy_net.5.ReLU.role: TRUE final 64-d policy latent (feeds action_net) -- the correct 'policy latent'

raw_action_params.role: action_net output before the policy transform

note: Closes the gap from build_r6c_frozen_test_rollout_for_joseph.py, which kept only the (mislabeled) penultimate latent + value latent. Here EVERY layer's pre- and post-activation is exported, plus the 1834-d input and the true 64-d final policy latent.
